steps.title: Find the yearly multiplier
steps.content: A $10\%$ depreciation means $90\%$ of the value remains each year. The multiplier is $0.90$.

steps.title: Apply the multiplier twice
steps.content: After $2$ years, multiply the original value by $0.90$ twice: $$ 600(0.90)^2=600(0.81)=486 $$ The answer is B.
